hazard.summary: TestColdStartLatency fails when a get-user-session operation exceeds a fixed 10s timeout (context deadline exceeded).
signals: internal error while retrieving user account memberships

constraints: Timeout budgets for get-user-session must exceed observed tail latency under CI cold-start conditions (avoid hard 10s caps at the boundary).
Timeout errors must include operation name, elapsed time, and configured timeout budget (present in evidence).
The test must not be fragile to expected CI tail latency; either reduce critical-path latency or increase the timeout budget with margin.
knobs: ci_parallelism_or_contention_controls (if CI load impacts tail latency)
verification: Re-run the linked GitHub Actions workflow and confirm TestColdStartLatency passes.
Confirm the prior signature string no longer appears in CI logs: "timed out after 10.001s (given timeout 10s)".
If the mitigation is a budget increase, confirm the new timeout has margin above tail latency (not barely passing).
notes: Type A receipt: evidence includes explicit timeout budget (10s), elapsed (10.001s), operation name, and the error chain in CI logs.
